forms.title: Startup Discovery
forms.description: A structured discovery interview to understand a startup idea, its problem space, market, and team readiness.

sections.title: Problem
sections.description: Understand the core problem the founder is trying to solve.
sections.summaryTemplate: The founder describes the problem as: {{answers}}. This section should be evaluated for clarity, evidence of pain, and urgency.
sections.aiPrompt: Analyze whether the described problem is specific, painful, and validated by real evidence rather than assumption.

questions.title: What problem are you solving?
questions.description: Describe the core problem in your own words.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Small businesses struggle to manage inventory across multiple sales channels...
questions.hint: Be as specific as possible. Avoid vague statements.
questions.questionPrompt: Evaluate the clarity and specificity of this problem statement.

questions.title: Who experiences this problem most acutely?
questions.description: Name the primary persona or segment.
questions.placeholder: e.g. Solo restaurant owners with 1-3 locations

questions.title: How confident are you that this problem is validated (not assumed)?
questions.description: Rate your confidence based on real customer conversations, not intuition.
questions.hint: 1 = pure assumption, 5 = validated with strong evidence

sections.title: Market
sections.description: Explore the size, competition, and positioning of the target market.
sections.summaryTemplate: Market analysis based on founder input: {{answers}}. Assess realism of market sizing and competitive awareness.
sections.aiPrompt: Assess whether the market description shows awareness of competitors and realistic sizing.

questions.title: Who are your main competitors or alternatives?
questions.description: Include direct competitors and indirect alternatives (e.g. spreadsheets, manual processes).
questions.placeholder: e.g. Competitor A, Competitor B, or manual Excel tracking...
questions.questionPrompt: Check if the founder distinguishes direct vs indirect competitors.

questions.title: How well do you understand your target market size?
questions.description: Rate your confidence in your TAM/SAM/SOM estimates.

sections.title: Team
sections.description: Assess the founding team's readiness and relevant experience.
sections.summaryTemplate: Team readiness summary: {{answers}}. Evaluate domain expertise and execution capacity.
sections.aiPrompt: Assess whether the team has relevant domain expertise and execution capability for this specific problem.

questions.title: What relevant experience does your team have?
questions.description: Domain expertise, past startups, technical skills, etc.
questions.placeholder: e.g. 5 years in logistics, previously built a SaaS product...

questions.title: How committed is the team to working on this full-time?
